rows.name: January
rows.overview: January Barometer predicts year's course with .733 batting average (84.0% accuracy for major calls). Every down January on the S&P since 1950 preceded a new or extended bear market, flat market, or 10% correction.

rows.name: February
rows.overview: February is the weak link in the "Best Six Months" (Nov-Apr). S&P ranked #11 overall.

rows.name: April
rows.overview: April is the #2 Dow month (avg +1.8%) since 1950. Up 16 straight years from 2006 to 2021.

rows.name: May
rows.overview: "Sell in May" - worst six months begin. $10,000 invested Nov-Apr compounded to $1,316,635 over 75 years vs only $4,253 for May-Oct.

rows.name: June
rows.overview: June is the worst midterm month - dead last (#12) for Dow, S&P, and NASDAQ. Midterm S&P averages -2.1%.

rows.name: July
rows.overview: July is the best month of Q3 and ranked #4 for both Dow and S&P. Start of 2nd half brings retirement fund inflows.

rows.name: August
rows.overview: August is the worst Dow month and 2nd worst S&P/NASDAQ since 1988 (harvesting made it the best month 1901-1951). First 9 trading days are historically weak.

rows.name: September
rows.overview: September is THE WORST month - ranked dead last (#12) across ALL five major indexes since 1950. Portfolio managers back from Labor Day clean house.

rows.name: October
rows.overview: October is THE PIVOT MONTH. Despite Octoberphobia (1929, 1987 crashes), October is a "Bear Killer" that turned the tide in 13 post-WWII bear markets.

rows.name: November
rows.overview: Month Rank (since 1950): DJIA #1, S&P 500 #1, NASDAQ #2 (since 1971). Average % Change: DJIA +1.9%, S&P 500 +1.9%, NASDAQ +2.1%.

rows.name: December
rows.overview: Month Rank (since 1950): DJIA #3, S&P 500 #3, NASDAQ #3 (since 1971). Average % Change: DJIA +1.5%, S&P 500 +1.4%, NASDAQ +1.5%.

rows.label: January Barometer
rows.description: As S&P goes in January, so goes the year. 84.0% accuracy since 1950 with only 12 major errors in 75 years. Devised by Yale Hirsch in 1972. Including flat-year errors yields 73.3% accuracy. Full years followed January direction in 11 of last 19 midterm election years.

rows.label: First Five Days Early Warning
rows.description: Last 48 up First Five Days followed by full-year gains 83.3% of time, avg 14.2% gain in all 48 years. 27 down First Five Days followed by 15 up and 12 down (44.4% accurate), avg gain 1.1%. Poor record in midterm years: only 9 of 19 followed direction.

rows.label: January Indicator Trifecta
rows.description: Combines Santa Claus Rally, First Five Days, and January Barometer. When all three positive: S&P up 90.6% of time (29 of 32 years), avg gain 17.7%, next 11 months up 87.5% (28/32), avg 12.6%. When all three down: S&P avg loss -3.6% with bear markets in 1969, 2000, 2008. When preceded by prior-year bear: always positive (14-0), avg full-year gain 22.2%.

rows.label: Best Three-Month Span Ends
rows.description: November, December, and January constitute the best three-month span with 4.4% S&P gain. January NASDAQ particularly powerful at 2.6% avg since 1971.

rows.label: Midterm January Weakness
rows.description: Midterm year Januarys are significantly negative: Dow -0.7%, S&P -0.9%, NASDAQ -0.7%, Russell 1K -1.1%, Russell 2K -1.4%. Contrasts sharply with overall January avg of +1.1% S&P. January ranked #1 for NASDAQ overall but drops to negative in midterm years.

rows.label: January Effect / Small Cap
rows.description: January Effect favors small-cap stocks but now starts in mid-December. Small caps outperform large caps from mid-Dec through early March. Tax-loss selling creates bargains in beaten-down small stocks.

rows.label: February Weak Link in Best Six Months
rows.description: February is the weak link in the Best Six Months (Nov-Apr). S&P ranked #11 overall. Recent record decent at +0.9% over last 15 years. Midterm year February slightly positive but unremarkable.

rows.label: Down Januarys Warning
rows.description: Every down January since 1950 was followed by a new or continuing bear market, a 10% correction, or a flat year. Down Januarys were followed by substantial declines averaging -13.3%, providing excellent buying opportunities later in most years. January Barometer 84.0% accurate.

rows.label: Presidents' Day Pattern
rows.description: Day before Presidents' Day weekend S&P down 21 of 34, 11 straight years 1992-2002. Day after mixed: up 18, down 16. NASDAQ down 19 of last 31 day after, but up 8 of last 13.

rows.label: February OpEx Day Bearish
rows.description: February options expiration day is one of the most bearish OpEx days in the calendar. S&P down 28 of 46 on a D6 streak, avg -0.17%. NASDAQ down 28 of 46 on a D4 streak, avg -0.33%. Week after OpEx also negative: S&P avg -0.44%, NASDAQ avg -0.50%.

rows.label: Midterm Bottom Pickers Paradise
rows.description: In last 16 quadrennial cycles since 1961, 10 of 19 bear markets bottomed in the midterm year. Bottoms often occurred in an air of crisis. Since 1914 Dow gained 46.3% avg from midterm low to pre-election high.

rows.label: Midterm Year Cycle
rows.description: Since 1914, Dow gained 46.3% avg from midterm year low to pre-election year high. 2026 is a midterm year. Post-election high to midterm low averages -20.2%. Dow 2025 high 44882 implies potential 2026 low ~35815.

rows.label: December Low Indicator
rows.description: If Dow closes below its December closing low in Q1, watch out. 36/38 occurrences since 1952 saw additional avg decline of 10.9%. At press time Dow breached Dec low and fell 10.2% further. 22 of 38 times rest-of-year gained after low was set.

rows.label: Best Months Switching Strategy
rows.description: 75-year track record. Best months = invested in equities (DIA, SPY, QQQ, IWM). Worst months = Treasury bonds (SGOV, SHV, IEF, TLT). Start looking for MACD SELL signal on April 1 for Dow and S&P.

rows.label: March Late-Month Weakness
rows.description: Last 3-4 days of March: Dow net loser 22 of last 36 years. Week after Triple Witching Dow down 23 of last 38. End-of-quarter rebalancing creates selling pressure.

rows.label: March OpEx Week Strength
rows.description: Options expiration week in March: Dow up 30 of 45, S&P up 30 of 46. Avg gain +0.52% Dow, +0.51% S&P. Monday before triple witching Dow up 27 of 38.

rows.label: Best Six Months Ends
rows.description: The 'Best Six Months' (Nov-Apr) ends in April. Start looking for MACD SELL signal on April 1 for Dow and S&P. When triggered, switch equity exposure to Treasury bonds or defensive positions for May-October.

rows.label: 4-Year Cycle Sweet Spot Setup
rows.description: Q2-Q3 of midterm year 2026 is historically the weakest period: Dow -2.0%, S&P -2.5%, NASDAQ -6.6% avg. But this sets up the Sweet Spot from Q4 2026 to Q2 2027 where Dow gains 19.3%, S&P +20.2%, NASDAQ +29.4%. Look for late Q3/early Q4 low (Aug-Oct) as buying opportunity.

rows.label: April OpEx Week Historically Strong
rows.description: April options expiration week: Dow up 35 of 46 (76%), avg +0.89%. S&P up 30 of 46, avg +0.68%. Week AFTER opex also strong: S&P up 30 of 46, avg +0.49%. Current OpEx week streak D4 for S&P (down last 4 years).

rows.label: Post-Tax-Deadline Strength
rows.description: April exhibits strength after the tax deadline (Apr 15) in recent years. Earnings season anticipation drives pre-report rallies.

rows.label: Midterm Year April Weakness
rows.description: In midterm election years since 1950, April averages are weaker: Dow +0.4%, S&P -0.3%, NASDAQ -1.1%. 2026 is a midterm year - expect below-average April performance vs all-years average.

rows.label: Good Friday Effect
rows.description: NASDAQ up 21 of last 25 days before Good Friday (Apr 3, 2026). Day after Easter (Apr 6) is second worst post-holiday trading day.

rows.label: Worst Six Months Begin
rows.description: Worst six months of the year begin with May. $10,000 compounded to $1,316,635 for Nov-Apr vs only $4,253 for May-Oct over 75 years. MACD timing triples Best Six results to $3,743,209.

rows.label: Summer Volume Doldrums
rows.description: After Memorial Day, trading volume declines toward summer low. Anemic volume and uninspired trading. Summer rallies tend to be short-lived, quickly followed by pullback or correction. Difficult to sell at good prices.

rows.label: Midterm May Weakness
rows.description: Midterm year Mays: Dow -0.6%, S&P -0.7%, NASDAQ -0.8%. Ranks #8 Dow, #9 S&P. Part of the historically weak Q2-Q3 midterm period.

rows.label: May OpEx Week Negative
rows.description: May OpEx week: Dow down 24 of 45, S&P down 24 of 45. Avg -0.08% Dow, -0.06% S&P. One of the weaker OpEx weeks. Week AFTER is better: S&P up 30/45, avg +0.40%.

rows.label: Memorial Day Week Mixed
rows.description: Dow down 17 of last 29. Was up 12 straight 1984-1995 but bearish trend since. Day after Memorial Day Dow up 23 of 39 overall but down 8 of last 10.

rows.label: NASDAQ MACD Sell Watch
rows.description: Start looking for NASDAQ MACD Sell Signal on June 1. NASDAQ's Best Eight Months end later than Dow/S&P.

rows.label: Post-Triple-Witching June Crash Zone
rows.description: Week after June Triple-Witching: Dow down 29 of last 35 (83%), avg loss 0.8% since 1990. One of the most reliably bearish weeks in the entire calendar. Falls on June 22-26 in 2026.

rows.label: NASDAQ Best Eight Months Ends
rows.description: June ends NASDAQ's Best Eight Months (Nov-Jun). $10,000 invested in best 8 months since 1971 gained $1,569,428 vs $996 in worst 4 months. MACD timing triples to $3,487,640. After June, NASDAQ enters its weakest Jul-Oct void.

rows.label: Summer Rally Weakest of Four Seasons
rows.description: The summer rally is the weakest rally of all four seasons. Summer doldrums begin in late June. Volume declines, rallies short-lived.

rows.label: Midterm June Severely Negative
rows.description: Midterm year Junes: Dow -1.9%, S&P -2.1%, NASDAQ -1.9%, Russell 2K -2.1%. Ranks dead last (#12) for Dow, S&P, and NASDAQ. Deep in the 4-Year Cycle weak spot.

rows.label: End-of-Quarter Portfolio Pumping
rows.description: Last day of June: watch for end-of-quarter portfolio pumping. Dow down 19 of last 34, but NASDAQ up 10 of last 14. Mixed signal.

rows.label: Best 6 + 4-Year Cycle Strategy
rows.description: Only 4 trades every 4 years triples Best Six results to $4,597,352 on $10,000. In midterm years: Sell May-Oct, Buy Nov-Apr. Then hold from midterm Oct MACD buy through post-election Apr MACD sell (~2.5 years). 2026 midterm: sell in spring, buy on MACD signal Oct 2026.

rows.label: July First Trading Day Powerhouse
rows.description: First trading day in July: S&P up 32 of last 36 (89%), avg gain 0.5%. One of the single strongest trading days in the calendar year. Driven by 2nd-half retirement fund inflows.

rows.label: July Front-Loaded Strength
rows.description: July strength concentrated in first half. Graph shows gains front-loaded then fading. Huge gain in July usually provides better buying opportunity over next 4 months. NASDAQ enters worst 4 months (Jul-Oct).

rows.label: Midterm July Split Personality
rows.description: Midterm July: Dow +1.6% (#3), S&P +1.3% (#3), but NASDAQ -0.8% (#7), Russell 2K -2.5%. Large-caps hold up while small-caps and tech suffer. IRIS should note this divergence for SPY vs broader market.

rows.label: July OpEx Day Bearish
rows.description: July OpEx day: Dow down 24 of 43, avg -0.20%. S&P avg -0.23%. NASDAQ avg -0.35%, down 27 of 45. NASDAQ OpEx week D7 streak (down 7 straight years).

rows.label: Post-July-OpEx Volatile
rows.description: Week after July OpEx prone to big swings: Dow up 17 of 23 but with massive individual moves (+3-4% and -3-4%). High volatility zone.

rows.label: Super-8 Days Pattern
rows.description: Market gains more on Super-8 days (last 3 + first 2 + middle 3 trading days) than all 13 remaining days combined. S&P Super-8 avg 0.56% per period vs 0.03% rest of month. 401(k) inflows drive mid-month strength.

rows.label: First Month of Quarter Bullish
rows.description: First month of Q1-Q3 (Jan/Apr/Jul) produces greatest gains historically. S&P Q3 first month avg +1.3%. Institutional annual/semi-annual/quarterly operations drive inflows.

rows.label: August First 9 Days Weak
rows.description: First 9 trading days of August historically weak. Dow first trading day down 19 of last 28. Combined with summer doldrums, early August is a danger zone. Midterm Aug: Dow -0.7%, S&P -0.4%, NASDAQ -1.4%.

rows.label: August Worst Dow Month Since 1988
rows.description: Harvesting made August best month 1901-1951. Now worst Dow and 2nd worst S&P/NASDAQ month since 1988. Saddam 1990 -10%, Russia crisis 1998 -15.1%. Best gains (1982, 1984) came as bear markets ended.

rows.label: Mid-August Stronger
rows.description: Mid-August stronger than beginning and end. OpEx week Mon has S&P 66.7. Week after OpEx also decent: S&P up 30/45, avg +0.34%.

rows.label: August 27 Anomaly
rows.description: 3rd-to-last trading day (Aug 27): S&P was up 19 years in a row 2003-2021. D 90.5, S 90.5, N 81.0 - the HIGHEST scores in the entire database. But next-to-last day (Aug 28) S&P down 19 of 29.

rows.label: Down Friday/Down Monday Warning
rows.description: DF/DM pattern: down Friday followed by down Monday has corresponded with market inflection points for 30+ years. Avg 10 occurrences per year, avg subsequent -6.0% Dow loss over next 3 months. Bear years average -12.5%. Use as early warning for IRIS.

rows.label: Q4 Sweet Spot Approaching
rows.description: Fourth-quarter market magic: Q4 provides greatest and most consistent gains. Midterm Q4 is the start of the Sweet Spot. Best 2-quarter span: midterm Q4 to pre-election Q1 averages Dow +13.8%, S&P +14.4%, NASDAQ +19.5%. The Oct MACD buy signal is ~2 months away.

rows.label: Summer Rally Weakest
rows.description: Summer rally (May/Jun low to Q3 high) averages only 9.5% - weakest of all 4 seasonal rallies. Winter 12.9%, Spring 11.5%, Fall 11.3%. Don't chase summer bounces.

rows.label: September Worst Month Overall
rows.description: Biggest % loser on S&P, Dow, and NASDAQ since 1950. Rank #12 across ALL five indexes. Midterm Sep: Dow -1.2%, S&P -0.8%, NASDAQ -1.6%, Russell 1K -1.8%. Portfolio managers back from Labor Day clean house. Down big in 4 of last 5 years.

rows.label: Post-Sep-Triple-Witching Carnage
rows.description: Week after September Triple Witching: Dow down 26 of last 35 (74%), avg loss 1.1%. S&P down 31 of 45, avg -0.81%. NASDAQ avg -0.90%. Russell 2K down 30 of 45, avg -1.44%. The WORST post-OpEx week in the calendar. Falls Sep 21-25 in 2026.

rows.label: September Mid-Month Bright Spot
rows.description: Sep 9-11 shows surprising strength: Wed S 71.4, Thu D/S 81.0 (bull), Fri S 76.2 (bull). FOMC Wed Sep 16 also strong: D 76.2, S 71.4, N 81.0. The mid-month is the only safe zone in September.

rows.label: September End-of-Quarter Weakness
rows.description: End of September prone to weakness from Q3 institutional portfolio restructuring. Last trading day S&P down 18 of past 28. S&P opened strong 18 of 30 years but tends to close weak.

rows.label: Fall Correction Season
rows.description: Fall slump (Aug/Sep high to Q4 low) averages -8.8%, the worst seasonal correction. 20 fall declines >10% since 1964. October jinx: 11 of those 20 did major damage in October. But most often pays to buy after Q4 waterfall declines for rally into Jan+.

rows.label: Midterm Bottom Watch Intensifies
rows.description: September is part of the Aug-Oct midterm bottom formation zone. The 4-Year Cycle MACD buy signal watch begins Oct 1. If Sep sells off hard (as midterm years typically do), the Oct bottom and Sweet Spot rally could be imminent.

rows.label: First Trading Day Phenomenon
rows.description: Dow gained 9002 pts on first trading days of 333 months (Sep 1997-May 2025) vs 24625 pts on remaining 6633 days. Avg 27.03 pts vs 3.71 pts. But Sep first days are net point losers. Aug first days worst (down 18 of 27 years).

rows.label: October Bear Killer
rows.description: October has turned the tide in 13 post-WWII bear markets: 1946, 1957, 1960, 1962, 1966, 1974, 1987, 1990, 1998, 2001, 2002, 2011, 2022. Despite Octoberphobia from 1929/1987 crashes, October is a great time to buy. Oct 2022 was best Dow month by points (+4000, +14.0%).

rows.label: MACD BUY Signal Watch Active
rows.description: Start looking for MACD BUY signals on October 1 for Dow, S&P, and NASDAQ. This triggers the Best Six Months entry (Nov-Apr) and in midterm years, the 2.5-year hold through the Sweet Spot. The MACD signal timing nearly triples Best Six results.

rows.label: Midterm October #1 Ranked
rows.description: Midterm year Octobers since 1950: #1 Dow (+3.2%), #1 S&P (+3.0%), #2 NASDAQ (+3.2%), Russell 1K (+3.9%), Russell 2K (+3.2%). THE BEST midterm month. The pivot from weak zone to Sweet Spot happens here.

rows.label: October OpEx Week Very Strong
rows.description: OpEx week: Dow up 32/45, avg +0.67%. S&P up 33/45, avg +0.68%. First day of OpEx week even better: Dow up 34/45, avg +0.66%. U3 streak across all indexes.

rows.label: Late October Buy Point
rows.description: Late October is time to buy depressed stocks, especially techs and small caps. The Almanac explicitly calls this a buying zone. Oct 22-23 show S&P 66.7-71.4 with bull icons - the late-month recovery.

rows.label: Sector Seasonality Launches
rows.description: Massive concentration of bullish sector seasonalities begin in October: Banking, Broker/Dealer, Computer Tech, Consumer Discretionary/Staples, Healthcare, Industrials, Materials, Pharmaceutical, Real Estate, Semiconductor, Telecom, Transports - nearly every sector goes long from October.

rows.label: Worst Six Months Ends
rows.description: October ends the Dow/S&P Worst Six Months (May-Oct) and NASDAQ Worst Four Months (Jul-Oct). The transition from worst to best seasonal period is the single most important inflection point in the calendar year.

rows.label: Best Six Months Begins
rows.description: November marks the start of the "Best Six Months" (Nov-Apr) for Dow and S&P 500. $10,000 invested only during Nov-Apr compounded to $1,316,635 over 75 years vs $4,253 for May-Oct. MACD timing nearly triples Best Six results. Also begins NASDAQ's Best Eight Months (Nov-Jun) and Best Three Months (Nov-Jan).

rows.label: November First Trading Day Strong
rows.description: First trading day of November: Dow up 12 of last 16. First trading days capture a disproportionate share of monthly gains. November 2026 first day (Mon Nov 2) has D/S/N all at 61.9 - uniformly bullish entry.

rows.label: Midterm Election Bullish Window
rows.description: Midterm election time is unusually bullish. Average 2.8% gain over 8 trading days surrounding midterm elections since 1934. November 3, 2026 is Election Day. Markets historically rally into and after midterm results as uncertainty resolves.

rows.label: November OpEx Week Strong
rows.description: November options expiration week: Dow up 27 of 45, avg +0.23%. OpEx day (Fri Nov 20): Dow up 28 of 45, avg +0.18%. Week after OpEx even stronger: Dow up 27 of 45, avg +0.60%.

rows.label: Thanksgiving Trade
rows.description: Buy Tuesday before Thanksgiving, hold until 2nd trading day of New Year. Russell 2000 up 76.1% of the time (35 of 46 years), avg gain 3.1%. One of the most reliable seasonal trades for small-cap exposure.

rows.label: Pre-Thanksgiving Week Weakness
rows.description: Week before Thanksgiving: Dow up 21 of last 32 overall but down 6 of last 8 - recent bearish trend. Wednesday before Thanksgiving (Nov 18) particularly weak with S&P 42.9. Trading strategy: long into weakness prior, exit into post-holiday strength.

rows.label: Santa Claus Rally
rows.description: Last 5 trading days of December plus first 2 trading days of January. S&P 500 avg gain 1.3% since 1950, 1.2% since 1969. In 2026: Dec 24 through Jan 4, 2027. Yale Hirsch's warning: "If Santa Claus should fail to call, bears may come to Broad and Wall." Santa's failure to show tends to precede bear markets or periods where stocks could be purchased at lower prices later in the year.

rows.label: Q4 Triple Witching Most Bullish
rows.description: December triple-witching week is the most bullish of all four quarterly expirations. S&P up 29 of last 41 during TWW. Triple-witching day itself (Fri Dec 18): S&P up 27 of last 43, but down 7 of last 10 - recent weakness despite long-term bullish record. Week after triple witching (Dec 21-24): Dow up 27 of last 34, avg gain 0.8%.

rows.label: January Effect Begins Mid-December
rows.description: Small caps start outperforming large caps from mid-December, not January. Tax-loss selling creates bargains in beaten-down small stocks. Russell 2000 vs Russell 1000 (38-year avg, Dec 1987-Feb 2025): 12/15-12/31 R2K +2.5% vs R1K +1.3%. The effect extends through February: 12/15-02/28 R2K +4.3% vs R1K +2.4%.

rows.label: Free Lunch Strategy
rows.description: Buy stocks hitting new lows on triple-witching Friday in December. Based on tax-loss selling overdoing the decline in thinly traded stocks. The "Free Lunch" list is compiled from new lows on Dec 18, 2026. Historically provides a quick bounce into January as selling pressure exhausts.

rows.label: December First Trading Day Weak
rows.description: December first trading day is surprisingly weak: Dow up only 46.7%, S&P 49.3%. NASDAQ fares better at 59.3% but has been down 7 of last 13. Contrasts sharply with November's strong first day (65.3%). Early December is generally the weakest part of the month before mid-month strength kicks in.

rows.label: Year-End Bearish Final Days
rows.description: Last trading day of the year: NASDAQ down 19 of last 25 - one of the most reliably bearish single days in the calendar. Dow and S&P also weak: D 33.3, S 28.6, N 28.6. The penultimate days (Dec 29-30) are also bearish: S&P 47.6 and 42.9. Tax-loss selling and portfolio window-dressing create late-December selling pressure before the Santa Claus Rally window resets.
